steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts modern technology — which lets searchers locate hoards before excavation — with pre-1960 discovery. The blank must describe how pre-1960 hoards were found: without intentional search tools, meaning discovery happened by chance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — include gold jewelry.: addresses the contents of hoards, not how they were discovered. ✗
  • Choice B — result from artifacts being unearthed accidentally.: directly captures discovery without intentional search — the logical opposite of using technology to locate hoards before digging. ✓
  • Choice C — include rare items from the 10th century BCE.: like A, focuses on contents rather than discovery method. ✗
  • Choice D — occur before the ground was disturbed.: addresses when hoards were found, not the method of discovery. ✗
B is correct.
